meaning: adj.<美><加> 闪光的,耀眼的,眩目的
examples: Palm Springs has ritzy restaurants and glitzy nightlife.
Nevada now needs humdrum jobs more than glitzy casinos.
This caused some glitzy malls to close and vexed shoppers.
By the glitzy standards of deal making, moving money around the world for iTunes is mundane.
The theater is gone and there's a new and glitzy steakhouse in the building.
